Common Parameters in libml

params.Rd

The parameters below are commonly used throughout the libml package.

Arguments

truth

character(n) or factor(n). A vector of true class names. In most instances you will have to also pass a pos_class argument defining the positive/event class.

predicted

numeric(n). A numeric vector of class probabilities.

pos_class

character(1). Name of the "positive" or "event" class.

cutoff

numeric(1). A cutoff for the decision/operating point, predictions above which are considered the positive class.

main

character(1). Optional string for the plot title.

y_lab

character(1). Optional label for the y-axis.

alpha

numeric(1) in [0, 1]. The color transparency. See also ggplot2::alpha().

color

character(1) or integer(1). Specify the colors for lines, points, bar, box, or ROC.

col

character(1) or integer(1). Specify the colors for lines, points, bar, box, or ROC.

data

A tibble or data.frame object containing data for analysis. Should often contain a "Response" column indicating the response variable, often the grouping variable.

feats

character(n). A vector of features, usually column names of a data frame.

formula

A formula class object, specifying the model to be fitted (e.g. \(response ~ x_1 + x_2 + ... + x_n\)).

response

character(1). The column name to use as the response grouping. Expected to be a factor with 2 levels. If not a factor, response will be coerced to a factor (i.e. alphabetical order). Level 2 of the factor is considered the disease/case group, thus positive values for the statistic indicate up-regulation in Level2 compared Level1.

nboot

integer(1). The number of bootstrap estimates to perform.

r_seed

`integer(1). The value of the random seed if reproducibility is desired.

Developed by Stu Field.
